avoid.reason: The 77.45% implied probability is broadly consistent with the inflationary signals in the supplied CPI, energy, and rate markets; no credible 8-point macro edge.
The 28.45% implied probability is compatible with the zero-cut market after accounting for differing start dates; insufficient independent evidence for an 8-point edge.
The 15.4% implied probability is internally consistent with the broader rate-cut curve, and the supplied data do not support a threshold-sized edge.

The contract uses a custom two-consecutive-negative-quarter definition across a long horizon. Without current labor, credit, and leading-indicator data, a sufficiently precise 8-point edge is not defensible.

avoid.market: WTI September 2026 settlement above $77.99 on July 17
avoid.reason: No contemporaneous September WTI futures quote, options surface, inventory report, or volatility input was supplied, so the short-dated 65.5% probability cannot be independently validated.

notes: The supplied curve indicates an inflationary regime: markets heavily favor no Fed cuts, near-term oil thresholds imply elevated crude prices, and the broader CPI curve centers inflation near the mid-3% range. Only five markets passed the $50K volume and 10-cent maximum-spread filters. None had enough specific grounding to establish the required category edge without stretching.
